column1: shmiao: I don't know, apparently you are stronger than me too...
C[shmiao: lets see how it goes...
C[seanachain: it seems i made a big boo boo :(
C[shmiao: I thought I read out a sequence where I died... but I guess I was imagining things, I can't find it again...
C[shmiao: it did not email me that you had moved...
shmiao: that wasn't meant to happen...
shmiao: I had a very busy week at work
shmiao: good game, and sorry about that
C[shmiao: So what got you into go?
madhat5: i saw it in a manga once, and starting reading more about
madhat5: also saw a korean gangster movie on netflix centered around go
madhat5: i like it more than chess, its less rigid but so much harder i think, because its so open
shmiao: definitely more open than chess, simple rules, but very complex play. A win in a localized area, can mean a global loss.
madhat5: yea, that's why i like it, really focuses more on the player and how far ahead they can think
madhat5: the movie is the Divine Move
C[shmiao: Ill check it out on netflix when I get home
shmiao: was the manga called hikaru no go?
madhat5: no, i didn't even know about that one, definitely going to read it now though (it's also on hulu apparently). I forget what the manga i read that had it, but it was just a scene in the manga

C[shmiao: when playing go, try to remember 3 things which should improve your play considerably. In order of importance 1. Are any of my groups in danger (can I save them?) 2. Are any of my opponents stones in danger (How can I put pressure on them?) 3. Whats the biggest move?
C[madhat5: Nice, thanks for the tips!!
shmiao: you're entire bottom group is dead too
madhat5: yea :/ i was trying to win that one group on the bottom right and totally screwed it up....
C[shmiao: it happens... your opening is good, just remember the 3 rules
shmiao: you should get this book, if you really want to learn and get good
madhat5: ...that group on the top right is eternal ko isn't it?
shmiao: it is ko. but you didnt need it to be
shmiao: whoever gets last play can fill in middle space
shmiao: last play in ko fight that is... but its not that important here. prob more important for me than it is for you. but in this case I dont need to win
shmiao: i can make life on right side pretty easy, or escape in corner, if you cut me and want to make life there
shmiao: might be hard but probably doable
shmiao: the problem with the way you played, is that now you have to respond to me cause if I cut you at p16 it will hurt
shmiao: so either you win the ko fight, or you block at p16
C[shmiao: now i am not in any immediate danger
shmiao: but I have bad shape
C[shmiao: why did you play s2?
shmiao: tell me to just shut up, if you prefer to play without my commentary
shmiao: yea, the bottom right corner is solid, there is no way I can invade that. It is your territory.
shmiao: ill be quiet now, so we can just play
madhat5: honestly, i was going to try to setup some eyes there, but realised after i didn't really need to
shmiao: you forfeiting, or just busy?
shmiao: what, you have more important things to do than play go?
madhat5: hehehe my bad, was a busy day at the office
madhat5: i'm doing tech support for an app called Qapital (https://www.qapital.com/)
madhat5: what're you up to these days?
C[shmiao: interesting, looking at their website now. How is it different to Mint?
shmiao: I am still working as a research in structured finance for an asset manager
madhat5: Mint is definitely a competitor. With us you can link your account with other apps like IFTTT, Apple Health and what not
madhat5: its goal and rule oriented, and we try to make the user enjoy savings
C[madhat5: yea its been good overall so far i think
C[shmiao: thats good, overall positive experience is good
C[madhat5: if i play g11, wouldnt that mean death for your group?
C[shmiao: no but you can atari at H10 to force me to play g11
C[shmiao: but I can respond with g11 or take your stones at d12
madhat5: yea, but i would still lose the group at f/g12
shmiao: I consider them dead already :)
madhat5: yea i saw that too :/
shmiao: heres a question, do you think your group at bottom is alive or dead?
madhat5: i think still alive, barely though
shmiao: its dead by the way. But I want to make sure you know why.
shmiao: All your eyes are called false eyes
madhat5: ohh man, i read about those too.. totally forgot, damn...
shmiao: haha, yea, they are tricky
C[shmiao: example is if you play L2
C[shmiao: now if you fill at k1 i play H1
shmiao: if you play H1 first I can take at K1
madhat5: i'm screwed either way, you would play at either e1 or g2 right?
C[shmiao: actually truth be told, if you did play anywhere at the bottom now, I would likely ignore, and play somewhere where I can score big points. I could probably ignore 1-3 moves of yours.
madhat5: yea that's yours now anyway

C[shmiao: If you remember at some point, I started playing elsewhere. Simply cause there was no way you could make life, and ignored many moves of yours down there. Allowed me to make big moyo on top left
shmiao: a little too far of a jump in my opinion, but you need to focus on another group
shmiao: I would have either played to help your weak group at C10
shmiao: or secured corner at R17
shmiao: but putting pressure on my right could also be good
shmiao: cause if I ignore you, I can make this really annoying move
C[shmiao: or if I played R17 would be really annoying for you
shmiao: but now you might really struggle to make life on left
shmiao: A9 is called a monkey jump
shmiao: and I hate it when people do it to me
shmiao: I will let you try to figure out the best way to respond
shmiao: if you havent checked it out already, I recommend http://senseis.xmp.net/?PagesForBeginners
shmiao: good place to learn stuff about go in general
madhat5: haha i actually always have that page open when i play
C[shmiao: good response by the way
C[shmiao: coudnt play at all this weekend...
madhat5: no prob! same here, hectic weekend/monday
C[madhat5: gonna try and start this one up again!
madhat5: but might just be a new game, so lookout for a challenge soonish
